label: Localized impetigo without cellulitis or systemic symptoms
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized impetigo.
No cellulitis, abscess, ecthyma or deep infection, eye/genital involvement, systemic illness, immunocompromised-host pathway, or outbreak/public-health-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Cellulitis, abscess, ecthyma, deep skin infection, systemic illness, sepsis, or rapidly spreading infection.
Eye, genital, extensive, bullous, recurrent, or immunocompromised-host disease.
Public-health-directed outbreak plan or poor follow-up.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or dermatology if symptoms are not improving with treatment or if spread continues.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus and CDC support impetigo framing, contagious superficial bacterial skin infection, red or pimple-like sores with crusting, spread prevention, clinician-prescribed antibiotic treatment, and escalation for spreading infection or worsening symptoms. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized impetigo without cellulitis, abscess, ecthyma, deep infection, eye/genital involvement, systemic illness, immunocompromised-host pathway, public-health-directed outbreak plan, or poor follow-up.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus and CDC support impetigo framing, contagious superficial bacterial skin infection, red or pimple-like sores with crusting, spread prevention, clinician-prescribed antibiotic treatment, and escalation for spreading infection or worsening symptoms. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized impetigo without cellulitis, abscess, ecthyma, deep infection, eye/genital involvement, systemic illness, immunocompromised-host pathway, public-health-directed outbreak plan, or poor follow-up.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized impetigo
no cellulitis, abscess, ecthyma, deep infection, systemic illness, eye/genital involvement, extensive disease, or immunocompromised-host pathway
